pages.raw_text:
Judge Edward Whitson today
took the case of Joseph Figlewski
against the Great Northern railroad
from the jury and set it over until
the next term of the federal court
because the complaint did not state
sufficient cause of action. Time is
given to prepare another complaint
Suit was brought for $4,000.


FiglewsN alleges that his wife,
who came from the east with their
children, was made insane by a
heartless conductor who threatened
to put her off the train If she did
not pay for the passage of one of
the children, for whom she had no
ticket. The woman, while out of
her mind threw her babe out Of a
car window.


The court held that insofar as the
action of the conductor was con-
cerned he had a right to tell her he
would put her child off.
